codeReview.summary: Reviewed original failed commit d9ed049 and fix commit ca6cf6b. The prior blocker (December `year + 1` overflow path in `range_end()`) is addressed by constraining parsed/constructed Year/Month/Day horizons to 1..=9999, making the month rollover arithmetic safe in reachable paths. Added boundary tests cover max-year December behavior and out-of-range rejection. No blocking regressions found in the fix diff.

issues.description: `new_datetime(dt)` does not validate the year range, while parse/docs and internal invariants now frame horizon years as 1..=9999 (`parse` docs at line 182; inner invariant comment at line 103). This does not reintroduce the overflow blocker, but it leaves constructor behavior inconsistent with the stated invariant model.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill requires explicit manual verification (including `cargo doc -p sd-core --no-deps`), but this fix handoff marks `followedProcedure: true` while providing no interactive/manual checks.

summary: PASS: the fix resolves the prior blocking overflow path by constraining Year/Month/Day horizons to 1..=9999 and adding boundary tests, with no new blocking defects identified in reviewed diffs.
